mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0 percent, it signals a labor market inflection that reduces recession risk premiums, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly contraction in real GDP and a monthly decline in retail sales indicate a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of co-contraction events, or the joint signal's information coefficient fails to exceed 0.05 in any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recoveries, a rebound in the industrial production index reflects capacity utilization normalization, which expands profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power when real GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts consistently lead equity market appreciation over short horizons because construction activity serves as a high-frequency leading indicator of consumer spending and business investment.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of months with rising housing starts, or the rolling Spearman correlation between HOUST changes and SPX returns falls below 0.10 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215, it confirms entrenched inflation that forces prolonged monetary tightening, which discounts future earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 215, or the signal's predictive accuracy degrades to chance levels during disinflationary recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly job growth exceeding 100,000 and a declining unemployment rate validate a broad-based economic expansion, reducing risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of confirmed labor market improvements, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansions, a divergence where industrial production rises while unemployment remains elevated indicates productivity-led growth without wage inflation, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while UNRATE stays above 6.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in over 60% of productivity-divergence episodes, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 0.5% over any 12-month rolling period.
